untranslatable_reason: Signal requires macroeconomic indicators not in allowed list: 'Share buyback activity > 7-year median' (no buyback data indicator), 'High-yield credit spreads < 600bps' (no HY spread indicator), 'Bank lending sentiment >= neutral' (no lending sentiment indicator), 'Equity valuations elevated relative to 5-year average' (no valuation ratio indicator), 'Fed tightening begins' (no Fed policy indicator), 'Yield curve inverts' (no yield curve indicator). This is a regime-detection signal requiring macro/fundamental data unavailable in technical indicator set.
translation_notes: The signal describes a regime change strategy based on structural market conditions (buyback activity, credit spreads, Fed policy, valuations) and leading indicators of regime reversal. These are primarily macroeconomic and sentiment-based signals that cannot be adequately represented using only the provided technical indicators. A proper implementation would require external data feeds for corporate action metrics, credit spreads, Fed policy calendar, and yield curve data.
